data.title: Chromoendoscopy to Detect Early Synchronous Second Primary Esophageal Carcinoma in Patients With Squamous Cell Carcinomas of the Head and Neck?
data.brief_summary:
Patients with HNSCC represent a high-risk group for the development of SESCC. Thus, esophagogastrofibroscopy should be performed to detect possible synchronous esophageal carcinomas in these patients.

Although only two patients with synchronous primary carcinomas were found among the patients with newly diagnosed HNSCC in this study, esophagoscopy and better some of advanced endoscopic methods should be recommended after detection of HNSCC to exclude secondary esophageal carcinoma or dysplasia.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Newly diagnosed patients with head and neck carcinoma
* Age 18-65

Exclusion Criteria:

* Recurrent head and neck carcinomas
* Previously diagnosed oesophageal cancer
endpoints: To detect early synchronous second primary esophageal carcinoma
